classObjective: Write equations for lines parallel or perpendicular to a given line through a specified point and determine whether two lines are parallel, perpendicular, or neither by comparing their slopes.
workedExamples: 5-2, Example 7 — Write Equations for Parallel Lines Through a Given Point
5-2, Example 8 — Write Equations for Perpendicular Lines Through a Given Point
5-2, Example 9 — Determine Whether Two Lines Are Parallel, Perpendicular, or Neither
warmUp: Find the slope of a line perpendicular to y = 3x - 7
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: negative reciprocal relationship; m_1 · m_2 = -1 check; justification arguments for parallel/perpendicular classification
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing parallel and perpendicular lines and classifying line relationships
independentPractice: Practice problems writing parallel/perpendicular lines through specified points and determining line relationships
assessment: Exit ticket: write the equation of the line perpendicular to y = -1/3 x + 5 passing through (2, 1)
capReflection: Why is the product of slopes equal to -1 for perpendicular lines?
